evals.prompt: I want to start working with Camunda 8 locally. I don't have a cluster yet. Help me get going.
evals.expected_output: Walks the user through installing c8ctl, starting a local cluster via c8ctl cluster start, and verifying the cluster is alive
expectations: Recommends starting a local cluster using c8ctl cluster start (the c8run wrapper) rather than telling the user to download c8run separately
Explains that c8ctl cluster start downloads and starts c8run on first run, defaulting to the latest stable
Verifies the cluster is alive (c8ctl cluster status or c8ctl get topology)
Does NOT require the user to manually create a profile for the local case (mentions the localhost fallback)

evals.prompt: Set up c8ctl to talk to my Camunda 8 SaaS cluster. Cluster URL is https://abc123.bru-2.zeebe.camunda.io and I have a client ID and secret from the Camunda console.
evals.expected_output: Adds and activates a c8ctl profile for the SaaS cluster with the provided credentials
expectations: Uses c8ctl add profile <name> with --baseUrl, --clientId, --clientSecret flags
Activates the profile with c8ctl use profile <name>
Verifies the connection (e.g., c8ctl get topology or c8ctl list pd)
Does NOT append /v2 to the SaaS cluster URL manually (c8ctl handles this for cloud URLs)
Does NOT hardcode credentials in shell history visibly (uses placeholders or recommends env vars / secret managers if appropriate)

evals.prompt: I have c8ctl installed and a local cluster running. How do I see what's currently deployed and what process instances are running?
evals.expected_output: Demonstrates the inspection commands for deployed definitions and running instances
expectations: Uses c8ctl list pd (or c8ctl list process-definitions) to show deployed process definitions
Uses c8ctl list pi (or c8ctl list process-instances) to show running instances
Optionally narrows results with --state=ACTIVE or filter flags
Optionally suggests c8ctl get topology for cluster-level info
Does NOT require the user to install additional plugins for this

evals.prompt: I want to install the c8ctl diagram-renderer plugin from https://github.com/camunda/c8ctl-plugin-diagram-renderer and use it to render a process instance diagram.
evals.expected_output: Installs the plugin and renders a diagram
expectations: Verifies the plugin is loaded (c8ctl list plugins or c8ctl help)
Mentions --output for saving to a file (since not all terminals support inline image rendering)
